name: Jordan Watkins
headline: Deep-roster flyer with no role and tiny 2025 sample
body:
There is almost nothing to build on here. Watkins logged just two games as a rookie, running 14 routes on a 10.2% snap share while drawing three targets for two catches and 26 yards. That is a 0.5% target share and a 0.015 WOPR, the profile of a player who barely sees the field. The lone encouraging crumb is efficiency on microscopic volume: a 0.331 EPA per play and 14.3 air yards per target, suggesting he was used downfield when he did play. None of it is predictive at this sample size.

The outlook for 2026 is buried depth. He enters camp fighting for a back-end spot in a crowded San Francisco receiver room, and Shanahan's praise for his conditioning is the kind of note that fills a roster, not a target tree. With no projections, no ADP, and roughly 0% rostered, he is a watch-list name only, relevant if injuries clear a path.
